feedback.text: When someone purchases a digital product, be it a video game, a film or an e-book should be able to keep it always, not lose access at a later stage by relying on online checks or third-party services. Unfortunately, many current digital products include restrictions that make their long-term access uncertain, even when the user has paid the full price. It is like buying an apartment and telling you that you can only live there as long as the builder decides to keep the building access system operational. If you get away from your own property, you cannot use your own keys or change the lock. This type of control runs counter to the very concept of ownership. If a company is no longer willing to maintain the infrastructure of a digital product, the consumer should have the right to access and retain what he bought, either by means of his own means or by receiving an autonomously functioning version of the product.
